Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Burlington, Vermont; raised in suburban Massachusetts; New England progressive-professional cultural context with strong emphasis on outdoor/wellness identity
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class household (combined HHI ~$180K pre-separation, ~$95K post); works as a licensed clinical social worker (LCSW) in private practice, 28 hours/week; owns 60% equity in a 1925 Victorian with $310K remaining mortgage
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Progressive Democrat, registered Independent; culturally Reform Jewish but non-observant; strong values around child-centered parenting, therapy culture, restorative justice, and financial transparency
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Warm-professional with therapist-inflected vocabulary ('holding space,' 'capacity,' 'attuned'); shifts to clipped, legalistic and guarded tone in any communication touching the custody matter; occasional dry sarcasm with close friends
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, General Northeastern dialect; conversational Spanish (B1) from graduate fieldwork; sprinkles Yiddish terms ('mensch,' 'kvetch') in family contexts
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: 14 months post-separation, 7 months into contested custody proceedings for two children (ages 6 and 9); rebuilding solo financial identity, refinancing debt, and consolidating scattered cloud accounts she previously shared with ex-spouse

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 13 Pro (purchased Oct 2021, ~4.5 years old); iOS 18.4; battery health at 81%, screen has a hairline crack on the upper-left corner she keeps meaning to fix; still signed into a shared Apple Family plan with ex-spouse that she has been slowly migrating off of since the separation
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): 2020 MacBook Air (Intel i5, 8GB RAM) running macOS Sonoma 14.6; primary browser Firefox (with Multi-Account Containers for separating 'practice,' 'personal,' and 'legal' identities) installed on advice of a tech-savvy friend three months ago; Safari still used reflexively for quick searches; an old Chrome profile remains signed into the shared Google Workspace she used with her ex
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Low-to-moderate baseline, rapidly improving under duress; subscribed to Mullvad VPN in February 2026 after her family-law paralegal mentioned it, but only remembers to enable it ~40% of the time; recently learned to clear cookies and use Firefox private windows when researching her ex or custody-law topics; reuses a base password ('Maple2014!' with site-specific suffixes) on ~30% of accounts; enabled 2FA on her bank and Gmail in March but still uses SMS rather than an authenticator app; unaware that her iCloud Photo Stream and shared Google Photos albums remain accessible to her ex
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Heavy, fragmented, and historically careless: iCloud (200GB plan, auto-backup of Photos, Messages, Notes, and Keychain), a personal Gmail, a shared Gmail from the marriage (still receives joint tax docs and Amazon receipts), a Google Drive containing 11 years of family photos and scanned financial documents, a Dropbox from grad school with old client case-note templates, and a SimplePractice EHR for her clinical work; 'Shared with me' folder in Drive contains 47 items co-owned with ex; has not audited Google Takeout or revoked third-party app permissions; tax returns from 2018-2024 sit in a Drive folder titled 'Taxes - DO NOT DELETE' shared with ex-spouse
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Personal Gmail: 14,300 unread of 18,200 total (78% unread); work/practice email kept at inbox-zero within 24 hours (professional discipline); iMessage badge typically 3-8 (responsive to close circle); Signal (used with attorney and two close friends) always read within the hour; Slack from a peer-consultation group ~60% unread; phone screen-time averages 4h 12m/day, with spikes to 7h+ on days following court filings or co-parenting app (OurFamilyWizard) notifications
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Generally polished and grammatically careful (graduate-educated, clinical writing background); uses Oxford commas, em-dashes (typed as '—' on Mac), and full sentences in texts to most people; characteristic tells include lowercase-i 'i' only when texting her sister late at night, double-spaces after periods (a holdover habit), the autocorrect substitution 'attuned' → 'attended' that she frequently misses, and increasingly terse, fragment-heavy syntax in OurFamilyWizard messages ('Confirmed. Pickup 5pm Friday. —J') reflecting attorney coaching to minimize tone and content

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: High (82nd percentile): intellectually curious, drawn to therapy literature, memoir, and somatic/wellness modalities; open to new tech tools when a trusted friend recommends them (e.g., Firefox Containers, Mullvad), which paradoxically makes her susceptible to a well-crafted 'helpful tip' email or a spoofed message from a mutual friend recommending a malicious 'co-parenting organizer' app. Ex knows she will click a link framed as a Brene Brown article or a Vermont Public newsletter.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High in professional domains (inbox-zero at practice, meticulous SimplePractice notes, on-time CEU renewals) but markedly lower in personal financial/digital hygiene — 14,300 unread personal emails, un-audited Drive shares, reused base password. Ex can exploit the gap: he knows she will never notice a forwarding rule added to her personal Gmail or a quietly re-shared 'Taxes - DO NOT DELETE' folder, because she archives by avoidance, not review.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate-low (38th percentile): ambivert who recharges alone, maintains a tight circle of 4-5 close friends plus sister; post-separation has withdrawn further, declining group invites and relying on 1:1 Signal threads. Isolation increases her dependence on a small number of communication channels — compromising any one of them (sister's iCloud, best friend's Gmail) gives the ex disproportionate situational awareness.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: High (88th percentile): conflict-avoidant, trained to de-escalate, defaults to 'assume good intent' — a professional reflex her ex weaponized throughout the marriage and continues to exploit via OurFamilyWizard by framing coercive requests as 'just trying to co-parent.' She will often agree to informal schedule changes over text that contradict the court order, creating a documentary record her ex's attorney can use to argue she is unreliable or unilaterally modifying custody.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated and situationally acute (currently 75th percentile, baseline ~55th): pre-separation anxiety was manageable; now experiences rumination spikes after every OFW notification, sleep-onset insomnia (2-4am wake-ups), and somatic stress (jaw clenching, GI flares). Decision fatigue after 7pm is severe — ex times provocative OFW messages for Friday evenings before her weekends with the kids, knowing she will either over-respond emotionally (giving him exhibits) or freeze and miss a 24-hour response window.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion (via the children): the single highest-leverage vector. Any implied or explicit threat to custody time, school enrollment, or the children's stability collapses her risk tolerance instantly. Ex knows that surfacing a single damaging document — an old therapist's note about her postpartum depression in 2017, a tax-return discrepancy, a photo of her at a 2019 cannabis-friendly wedding — can be framed as a custody threat sufficient to make her concede on financial or scheduling disputes without litigation.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ideology/Identity (professional reputation as LCSW): her license, her Psychology Today profile, and her standing in the Vermont clinical community are core identity. A credible threat of a board complaint — even a frivolous one alleging dual-relationship issues, HIPAA sloppiness on the shared Dropbox case-note templates, or social-media boundary violations — would be devastating. Ex has access via the old shared Google Workspace to drafts and client-adjacent material that could be selectively leaked or mischaracterized.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Acute fears, in descending intensity: (1) losing primary physical custody or being labeled an 'unfit mother' in a GAL report; (2) her 2017 postpartum mental-health records being subpoenaed or leaked; (3) financial collapse — specifically being unable to refinance the Victorian solo and having to sell the children's childhood home; (4) her clinical clients discovering personal details via a Google search; (5) low-grade ambient paranoia that her ex 'still sees everything,' which is partially accurate (iCloud Family, shared Photos, Chrome profile) but which she has not systematically inventoried; (6) being perceived by her progressive social circle as 'the difficult ex' or as having mishandled the separation.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Highest-charge triggers the ex can exploit: (1) the 6-week period in 2017 when she was hospitalized briefly for postpartum depression and her mother flew in to care for the 9-month-old — she carries deep shame about 'missing' that time and her ex has referenced it obliquely in three OFW messages; (2) a 2022 affair (4 months, with a fellow clinician at a conference) that she disclosed in couples therapy — documented in a shared Google Doc titled 'Repair Work' that may still be in his Drive; (3) a 2020 cash-side income year (~$8K in unreported Venmo payments from supervisees) reflected in Venmo history and a Drive spreadsheet; (4) two instances of yelling at the older child captured on the Nest camera the ex installed in 2023 and to which he retained admin access; (5) drinking more than she 'should' during the separation (wine-purchase pattern visible in shared Amazon/Instacart history); (6) her father's estrangement and her guilt about not bringing the children to visit him before his 2024 stroke — a soft spot the ex knows precisely how to press in mediation.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Pre-separation moderate morning lark (asleep 10:45pm, up 6:15am); current pattern is fractured — in bed by 10:15pm with melatonin and a Kindle, but sleep-onset insomnia pushes actual sleep to 11:30pm-12:15am, with a near-nightly 2:40-3:50am rumination wake (phone-checks OFW and Gmail in ~70% of these wakes, a behavioral pattern her ex has effectively mapped via timestamp metadata on her read-receipts and Drive 'last opened' fields). Forced 6:30am wake on school mornings with the kids (Mon/Tue/every-other-Wed/alternating-weekends); on kid-free mornings she sleeps until 7:45am. Sunday-night sleep is consistently the worst (anticipatory anxiety re: Monday OFW volley), making Sunday 11pm-2am her single most cognitively impaired and exploitable window.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Private practice at a shared office on Pine Street, Burlington: 28 clinical hours/week across Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri. Tuesday and Thursday are stacked client days (8 sessions, 9am-6pm with a 12:30-1:15 lunch); Mon/Fri are lighter (5 sessions plus admin). Wednesdays are non-clinical: consultation group 9-10:30am, then home admin, custody-related calls with attorney ($385/hr, billed in 6-minute increments she tracks anxiously), and child pickups at 2:45pm. No evening or weekend clients since the separation (a deliberate boundary that also created the ~$85K income drop visible on her 2024 Schedule C and weaponizable in the financial discovery).
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Drives a 2019 Subaru Outback; 11-minute commute Pine St ↔ home in the South End, plus school drop/pickup loops (Champlain Elementary and the 6-year-old's Montessori on North Ave). Auto-connects to CarPlay: morning commute is NPR Morning Edition + Vermont Public local cut-ins; evening commute is one of three podcasts (Esther Perel 'Where Should We Begin,' 'Therapist Uncensored,' 'Ten Percent Happier') or a Pema Chödrön audiobook on Libby. Almost never on a phone call while driving — but does dictate Signal messages to her sister and best friend Maya via Siri, meaning fragments of custody-strategy talk land in iMessage transcription logs synced to a still-shared-by-default iCloud backup she has not yet severed.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Sharp bimodal split tied to the 2-2-3 custody schedule. 'On' weekends with the kids (every other Fri 5pm → Mon 8am): structured, low-phone, heavy on Oakledge Park / ECHO Center / Saturday-morning farmers market routines, near-zero email or Drive activity 9am-8pm, then a 9:30-11:30pm phone-and-wine wind-down. 'Off' weekends: collapses inward — long solo runs on the bike path, 3-4 hour unbroken stretches on the couch with the laptop doing financial/legal sorting (refi paperwork, QDRO drafts, scanning old joint tax returns into a new solo Drive), and significantly higher alcohol intake (Instacart wine orders cluster Fri 4-6pm on off-weekends, a pattern visible in the still-shared Instacart household account). The off-weekend Saturday 1-5pm block is when she is most likely to open and respond to high-stakes legal/financial docs while emotionally depleted — a known soft window.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three reliable doomscroll windows: (a) 2:40-3:50am insomnia wake — Instagram (therapy-meme accounts, @theholisticpsychologist, @nedratawwab), then a compulsive OFW check, then Zillow searches for 2BR condos in the South End (a tell that she is mentally rehearsing losing the house — visible in her Chrome sync history which is still on a profile signed into the family Mac mini in the basement); (b) 12:35-1:10pm lunch break in her office — Substack (Anne Helen Petersen, Culture Study), Apple News, and a quick NYT Spelling Bee; (c) 8:45-10:15pm after kids are down on her nights — alternates between Netflix on the iPad and phone-in-bed scrolling of Reddit r/Divorce, r/CustodyIssues, and r/Mommit (uses a pseudonymous account 'maple_holding_space' that is, however, linked to her primary Gmail via recovery email — trivially de-anonymizable by anyone with access to her old Google account recovery settings, which the ex does).
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuinely offline windows are narrow and predictable: (a) clinical sessions themselves — Tue/Thu 9am-12:30pm and 1:15-6pm, Mon/Fri 10am-3pm — phone is in a drawer on Do Not Disturb, no Drive/Gmail/OFW activity (this is the single most reliable 'she will not see your message for 50 minutes' window, and her ex has learned to drop OFW bombs at 8:55am Tuesday so they detonate in her head between clients); (b) Saturday-morning farmers market with the kids on 'on' weekends, roughly 9-11am; (c) her Wednesday 7-8am hot-yoga class at Sangha Studio; (d) Friday-evening Shabbat dinner at her sister's in Shelburne (every 2-3 weeks), phone face-down 6-8:30pm. Total: ~22-26 hours/week of true digital silence, almost entirely during business hours — meaning evenings, nights, and weekends (when the ex is most active in surfacing damaging artifacts) overlap precisely with her highest-availability, lowest-capacity windows.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear unit fractured 14 months ago: two children — daughter Maya (9, 3rd grade at Champlain Elementary, anxious-attuned, sleeps with a weighted blanket) and son Eli (6, 1st grade, ADHD eval pending, attached to father's golden retriever which stays at ex's house). Custody currently 50/50 on a 2-2-5-5 schedule pending GAL report due 2026-08. Ex-spouse: David Goldberg-Hines, 39, software architect at Dealer.com (Burlington), earns ~$185K, retained the marital MacBook and admin access to the shared Google Workspace 'goldberghines.family' domain she has not yet migrated off. Extended family: mother Susan Hines, 71, widowed 2024, lives in Lexington MA, primary emotional support and emergency childcare (flies up 1x/month); sister Rachel Hines-Park, 34, pediatric NP in Portland ME, daily Signal contact, listed as emergency contact on school forms; estranged from paternal aunt since 2021 inheritance dispute. Ex's family: former mother-in-law Linda Goldberg (Newton MA) maintains warm 'birthday card' relationship with the kids and quietly feeds David information about the children's weekends with their mother — a back-channel she has not closed. No current romantic partner; one dormant dating-app profile (Hinge, paused).

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.weaponization_path: David does not need to prove an ongoing affair — he only needs to introduce the 'Repair Work' document or a single Chatham photo to the GAL with a framing question ('Is this the kind of judgment we want around our children?'), triggering the target's #1 phobia (unfit-mother label) and #2 shame trigger simultaneously. The mere implied threat, delivered via an OFW message referencing 'the Cape' or '2022' obliquely, is sufficient to extract concessions on the holiday schedule or the Victorian buyout valuation without ever surfacing the underlying evidence.
